reviews.review_title: Wow! Great for learning Hands-Only CPR
reviews.review_description: Multiple levels and environments for practicing and learning the steps of Hands-Only CPR. There are two modes in the App - “Learn Mode” and “Practice Mode.” The “Learn Mode” helps teach the user the recommended steps for  Hands-Only CPR and using AED devices. “Learn Mode” teaches you the steps and then “Practice Mode” allows you to test yourself and see how you scored. Choose from three different environments (Gym, Library, and Courtyard) to learn and practice in different real life situations.

This is a great way to learn CPR. Not only was it a fun experience, it is also an important tool for everyone to know. I feel prepared and confident for my ability to step in if the situation arises. Highly recommended for all ages!

I recently had the opportunity to participate in a virtual reality CPR training session, and I must say, it was nothing short of incredible. The experience was truly revolutionary in the realm of CPR education.

From the moment I put on the VR  Meta headset, I was transported to a lifelike scenario where every detail felt incredibly real. The interactive nature of the experience made me feel fully engaged and immersed in the learning process.

One of the most remarkable aspects was the ability to practice CPR techniques on virtual patients. The lifelike simulations allowed me to apply chest compressions and practice rescue breathing in a safe and controlled environment. The feedback provided in real-time helped me refine my technique and build confidence in my ability to perform CPR effectively.

Cannot get the controllers to work.  Hand tracking not working either for some reason.
